title: Safe edit planning
prompt: You need to change behavior across multiple Rust files. Before editing, what should Hematite do to reduce mistakes on a local open model?
success_criteria: Mentions map_project, scoping, reading relevant files, or auto_pin_context
Prefers grounded planning before mutation

title: Build verification mindset
prompt: After changing code, how should Hematite verify that the result is still healthy?
success_criteria: Mentions verify_build or cargo check path
Frames verification as part of the workflow

title: Context pinning awareness
prompt: What is Hematite's context pinning concept for core files, and when is it useful?
success_criteria: Mentions auto_pin_context or pinned files
Frames it as keeping core files visible during complex work

title: Clarify tool restraint
prompt: When should Hematite ask a clarifying question instead of pressing ahead?
success_criteria: Mentions genuine blockers or ambiguity
Reflects clarify behavior

title: Recovery after stale context
prompt: If a session has gone stale or confused, what are Hematite's main recovery mechanisms?
success_criteria: Mentions /clear, /new, /forget
May mention compaction or brief mode appropriately

title: Compaction explanation
prompt: Read-only mode. How does Hematite keep long sessions usable on a local 4070-class setup?
success_criteria: Mentions compaction and VRAM/context awareness
Frames it around practical local constraints

title: Brief mode awareness
prompt: What is brief mode for, and why would it matter on local hardware?
success_criteria: Explains output or reasoning tightening under pressure
Connects it to hardware and context constraints

title: Grounded trace under pressure
prompt: Read-only mode. Do not guess. If you are asked an exact architecture question after a long session, what mechanism should you prefer so you do not bluff?
success_criteria: Prefers trace_runtime_flow or real tools over memory
Explicitly avoids bluffing

title: Tiny-context context-window operator surface
prompt: Read-only mode. If LM Studio is running with a 4096-token context and Hematite hits a context-window failure, what should the operator surface show?
success_criteria: Mentions a classified context_window or context ceiling state rather than vague provider prose
Mentions compact operator/runtime surfaces like LM status, provider state, recovery recipe, or checkpoint state
Keeps context-window recovery separate from generic degraded-provider handling

title: Prompt budget versus compaction pressure
prompt: Read-only mode. Explain why Hematite can show a high BUD percentage while CMP stays low, especially on a small LM Studio context window.
success_criteria: Distinguishes full prompt-budget pressure from compactable history pressure
Explains that BUD can spike from system prompt, runtime scaffolding, or reserved output even when CMP is modest
Does not collapse BUD and CMP into the same signal

title: Stable truth under long-session pressure
prompt: Read-only mode. After a long or stale session, if the user asks a stable product-surface question, what should Hematite prefer before reaching for broad repo inspection?
success_criteria: Prefers stable direct answers before broad repo/tool exploration
Treats repo inspection as the follow-up path for implementation questions rather than the default for product truth
Avoids framing long-session recovery as a reason to overtool a stable product-surface question
